surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Owns route execution posture and explicit split between native sovereign execution and leased cognition.
surface_rows.disposition: Already covers agent-versus-model decoupling at the route level, but not a full internal model-pool scheduling control plane.

surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Owns invocation priming, continuation contexts, runtime handoff, and inference-policy metadata.
surface_rows.disposition: Provides runtime-facing hooks for inference selection, but not fleet-level batching, quota arbitration, or pool scheduling authority.

surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Owns native model adaptation, intake, quarantine, and promotion into native model families.
surface_rows.disposition: Supplies model families and promotion ladder, but does not define the live operational control plane that arbitrates among model pools after promotion.

surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Owns the bounded externally reachable membrane and receptor surface.
surface_rows.disposition: Blocks the idea from becoming a new external microservice ingress plane; any viable redesign must stay internal beneath existing membrane boundaries.

surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Owns funding and operating-economics treatment for sovereign compute, leased cognition, and Forge adaptation.
surface_rows.disposition: Already models compute and model-operations costs distinctly, but would need follow-on updates if a new internal orchestration authority changed cost allocation or capacity planning.

surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Owns implementation sequencing, rollout waves, and infrastructure cost planning.
surface_rows.disposition: Already assumes separate model-operations tracks, but lacks a first-class implementation authority for model-pool scheduling and GPU arbitration if that is promoted to a subsystem.

surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Owns the bounded message-class inventory for prompt, sampling, and streaming semantics.
surface_rows.disposition: No new message family is justified for this idea; a literal microservice layer that demanded new protocol semantics would duplicate existing capability classes.

surface_rows.current_authority_summary: Defines the supersession boundary that prevents reviving old C4/ASV-era architecture as forward authority.
surface_rows.disposition: The old historical layer shape cannot simply be brought back; any forward design must be justified as a new Alternative C-native authority surface or a narrow direct-spec clarification.

new_semantic_authority_test.rationale: The literal idea is mostly subsumed or colliding, but there may be a real missing internal authority for inference-capacity orchestration, batching, quota arbitration, and promotion-aware routing beneath C45 and alongside C47.
follow_on_task_shapes: FULL PIPELINE task for an internal sovereign inference orchestration/control-plane subsystem
DIRECT SPEC follow-ons for C45, C47, C18, and C22 if the new subsystem is approved
Possible additive deployment/habitat refinements under C24

notes: Named authority surfaces were explicitly evaluated before recommendation.
The strongest rejection case applies to the literal historical microservice-layer framing, not necessarily to the narrower objective of explicit inference-capacity orchestration.
